summary: Tested SalesForm customer field inline 'Crear nuevo cliente' button + CustomerForm modal store name personalization. Inline button works correctly with pre-filled name. CustomerForm modal still shows hardcoded 'Tienda A'/'Tienda B' instead of personalized 'Tricomar Pets'/'Tricomar Grow' from Settings context.
passed: When no customer matches, a 'Crear nuevo cliente "<name>"' button appears below the search (mirrors product UX)
Button text correctly includes the searched name (e.g. Crear nuevo cliente "ClienteTest1782432744")
Clicking the button opens the CustomerForm modal
Modal NOMBRE field is pre-filled with the searched name

failed.issue: Store <select> displays hardcoded 'Tienda A' and 'Tienda B' instead of personalized names 'Tricomar Pets' and 'Tricomar Grow' from Settings context
failed.evidence: Direct DOM inspection of <option> textContent returned: 'Tienda A | Tienda B | Ambas'. CustomerForm.js lines 100-101 use {settings.store_a_name || 'Tienda A'} but the fallback is always rendered, indicating settings.store_a_name / store_b_name are not populated when modal renders.

action_items: Investigate useSettings() inside CustomerForm: verify SettingsProvider wraps the tree so settings.store_a_name/store_b_name are available when modal mounts.
Check that GET /api/settings actually returns store_a_name='Tricomar Pets' and store_b_name='Tricomar Grow' and that SettingsContext stores them under those keys.
Consider passing store names as explicit props from SalesForm into CustomerForm as a safer fallback, or initialize SettingsContext with the values fetched on app load before opening modals.
notes: The final submit-customer click was blocked by modal overlay timing during the script run (test-side flake, not a product bug). The core verifications were completed via DOM inspection and visual screenshot before that.
